title: fix: stabilize transaction history pagination
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Fixes transaction history pagination by using the Tinybird event id tie-breaker with the timestamp cursor.
- Returns `cursor_event_id` in JSON usage rows while keeping CSV columns unchanged.
- Displays API key names by resolving `api_key_id`, with the returned key name as fallback.
- Clarifies that activity charts use UTC buckets while transaction rows use local time.

Checks:
- `npx biome check --write enter.pollinations.ai/src/routes/account.ts enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/components/activity/transaction-history.tsx enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/routes/index.tsx enter.pollinations.ai/test/account-usage.test.ts`
- `npx tsc --noEmit -p enter.pollinations.ai/frontend/src/tsconfig.json`
- `npm run decrypt-vars && npx vitest run test/account-usage.test.ts`
- `npm run typecheck`

gist.headline: A perfect ledger
gist.blurb: Flipping through your transaction history is now perfectly smooth. We squashed a pagination bug and added API key names so you always know exactly what your hive is up to.
gist.summary: Stabilized transaction history pagination by adding a Tinybird event ID tie-breaker to the timestamp cursor, and updated the UI to resolve and display API key names.
gist.impact: Paging through account usage is now perfectly reliable, and developers can instantly see which specific API key generated each transaction.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as an accountant or archivist, organizing a long, continuous scroll or ledger of transactions. A retro computer monitor showing a neat list of API keys.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, carefully organizing a long, glowing digital scroll of transaction records. A chunky retro monitor in the background displays a neat, lime green (#ecf874) list of API keys. Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm and nostalgic.
